entries.statement: Knowledge of risk assessment tools and techniques, including quantification methods, assessment frameworks, and continuous evaluation approaches.
Knowledge of model evaluation methodologies, including train/validation/test set design, cross-validation approaches, and evaluation metric selection criteria.
Knowledge of evaluation frameworks for specific AI modalities, including NLP metrics (BLEU, ROUGE, BERTScore), computer vision metrics, and domain-specific evaluation methodologies.
Knowledge of AI testing methodologies including adversarial testing, red-teaming, edge case construction, distributional shift testing, and structured failure mode analysis.
Knowledge of AI-specific attack and threat taxonomies, including adversarial examples, model evasion, model extraction, model inversion, membership inference, data poisoning, and prompt injection.
Knowledge of comprehensive AI evaluation frameworks, including functional performance testing, behavioral testing, red-teaming, fairness evaluation, robustness testing, and safety evaluation.
Knowledge of evaluation dataset design principles, including test set construction to avoid contamination, representative sampling methodologies, edge case coverage, and coverage gap measurement.
Knowledge of AI system documentation standards including model cards, system cards, datasheets for datasets, and evaluation evidence standards required for regulatory conformity assessment.
Knowledge of model risk management regulatory requirements including Federal Reserve SR 11-7, OCC guidance, and supervisory expectations for model governance and independent validation.
Knowledge of model validation methodologies including conceptual soundness review, outcomes analysis, benchmarking, sensitivity analysis, back-testing, and champion-challenger model approaches.

Knowledge of model inventory management practices including model tiering by materiality and complexity, risk classification, lifecycle tracking from development through retirement, and documentation standards.
Knowledge of enterprise risk management integration, including how data risk, AI risk, model risk, and technology risk roll up into enterprise risk taxonomy and board-level reporting.

Knowledge of AI risk governance and organizational structures, including defining AI risk management roles and responsibilities, establishing governance frameworks, and integrating risk management into organizational decision-making.

Knowledge of high-risk AI system compliance requirements including EU AI Act Annex III classification, prohibited practices identification, conformity assessment procedures, and technical documentation requirements.
Knowledge of trustworthiness evaluation methodologies including fairness assessment, bias detection and measurement, safety evaluation, resilience testing, and validation of AI system reliability.
Knowledge of risk prioritization and response strategy development including risk ranking methodologies, mitigation measure design, risk transfer evaluation, and residual risk documentation.

Knowledge of AI cost-risk analysis and benefit assessment including financial impact evaluation of AI errors, benefit-risk trade-off analysis, cost-effectiveness evaluation, and economic impact measurement.

Knowledge of supply chain and third-party AI risk management including assessment of third-party AI component risks, intellectual property protection, vendor risk evaluation, and supply chain resilience.

Knowledge of human oversight and AI explainability including human-AI teaming configurations, AI system explanation requirements, human supervisory control design, and capability/limitation documentation.

Knowledge of AI model monitoring and lifecycle management including pre-trained model monitoring requirements, continuous risk tracking, feedback incorporation systems, and post-market surveillance design.
